content: Fixed-timestep game loop: accumulator updates physics at a constant dt while rendering every RAF frame; clamps tab-switch spikes
Input manager with held-key state plus a short press buffer so taps just before a window still register (jump/attack feel)
AABB overlap test plus minimal-axis resolution that pushes a moving box out of a solid and zeroes velocity on the hit axis
Sprite animator that cycles named frame sequences from one sheet image with per-animation fps, looping and one-shot support
Pooled particle system: preallocated pool, burst spawner with random angle and speed, additive fade-out rendering, no GC churn
Screen shake with exponential decay: trigger with a power on impacts, apply as a canvas translate around all drawing
Hit-pause (freeze frames): briefly scale dt to near zero on heavy impacts so hits feel weighty; cheap and high-impact juice
Easing functions and a tiny tween runner: animate any object property over time with easeOutCubic/easeOutBack instead of linear snaps
WebAudio SFX without audio files: tiny synth that plays beeps/noise hits via oscillator and gain envelopes; unlocked on first input
Entity state machine with enter/update hooks and a state timer; prevents tangled if/else for idle/move/attack/hurt logic
Floating score/damage numbers: spawn rising, fading text at an event location; communicates impact without UI work
Timed cue/input/hit window manager for reaction windows, telegraphed traps, rhythm hits, boss tells, and QTE scenes
Nonblocking generated-media loader: start RAF immediately, load images/audio/videos in background, resolve sprites by key, and fall back visibly without freezing
Skippable video overlay for cutscenes, transitions, intro/fail/victory clips, with any-key skip and onended/onerror continuation
Normalized 0..1 room animation helpers for hero boxes, hazard paths, body-part threats, and debug overlay drawing
Compact ROOMS array runner for scripted rooms: cue, timing window, success/fail, advance/retry, and two-step sequences
HiDPI-crisp canvas: size the backing store to devicePixelRatio then scale the 2D context so you draw in CSS pixels and retina displays are not blurry
Deterministic seeded RNG (mulberry32) plus a buildGrid(w,h,seed) generator — generate large maps/mazes from a seed instead of inlining a huge literal 2D array
Robust keyboard input using e.code (layout-independent), listening on window, preventDefault for arrows/space, listeners registered once and clearable on restart
Feature-detected localStorage save/load with try/catch so a first load in private mode or with storage disabled never throws
On-screen touch buttons/d-pad that write the SAME key state object as the keyboard so update() reads one input source on phone and desktop
Grid BFS pathfinding (4-neighbour) returning the shortest path from start to goal — good for chasing enemies, maze navigation, and tower-defense creep routing
Image load race fix: start the RAF loop immediately and draw a visible MISSING fallback until each PNG decodes; never await inside the frame
Browser autoplay policy fix: AudioContext starts suspended until a user gesture — resume it on the first pointerdown/keydown so sound actually plays
Toggleable help/instructions overlay for canvas games; H or ? opens, Escape closes, and a visible button works for mouse/touch.
First-person mouse-look with Pointer Lock plus WASD movement; yaw/pitch from mouse delta, click-to-lock, Escape releases. Genre-free 3D/first-person camera input.
Scrolling camera that follows the player with a world->screen offset and clamping to level bounds; draw world entities translated by the camera. Genre-free for any world larger than the canvas.
Toroidal screen wrap: entities leaving one edge reappear on the opposite edge (asteroids-style). Wrap positions after integration, not before.
Generate an irregular convex-ish polygon (asteroid/rock/island) by jittering radius per angle from a seed, so shapes are NOT perfect circles. Reuse the same vertex list each frame.
Uniform-grid spatial hash for broad-phase collision so many entities don't cost O(n^2). Insert each entity into its cell, then only test entities sharing or neighboring cells.
